full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic propeller for an ISR (International Submarine Races) human-powered submarine, compliant with ISR 18 Contestant Manual v1.2. The pilot drives the propeller via a pedal crank at 120 RPM (design point). Propeller geometry: 3 blades, diameter 600 mm, hub diameter 60 mm, pitch-to-diameter ratio 1.2 (so pitch = 720 mm), blade root chord 100 mm, blade tip chord 40 mm, blade twist at root 45 deg, twist at tip 15 deg. Material: 6061-T6 aluminum (machined), E = 68.9 GPa, yield = 276 MPa, ultimate = 310 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3. Shaft interface: 25 mm diameter keyed bore, 8 mm key width. Rated continuous pilot power 300 W -> shaft torque = 300 / (2 pi x 120/60) = 23.9 N*m. Peak sprint power 600 W for 30 s -> 47.8 N*m. Load cases: (R1) Continuous operation: 23.9 N*m torque at 120 RPM; each blade root carries equal share; max von Mises at blade root fillet <= yield / 3.0 = 92 MPa (fatigue factor for continuous operation). (R2) Sprint peak: 47.8 N*m torque; max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 184 MPa. (R3) Hydrodynamic thrust: at 120 RPM and operating submarine speed 2 m/s, propeller generates thrust approximately 150 N, reacted through the hub onto the shaft; max von Mises at hub <= 92 MPa. (R4) Debris strike: a 200 N tangential impact at blade tip (simulating blade strike on pool debris) applied as transient quasi-static; max von Mises <= ultimate / 1.5 = 207 MPa. (R5) Centrifugal: at 2 x sprint RPM = 240 RPM (safety envelope), centrifugal tensile stress at blade root <= 92 MPa. (R6) Propeller mass <= 1.5 kg. (R7) First modal frequency cantilevered at hub bore >= 100 Hz (>>2x blade-passing frequency 6 Hz at continuous operation). Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x4 cases + centrifugal body load) + modal for R7. Vibration secondary via R7. Thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required (the hydrodynamic thrust is pre-reduced to an equivalent force). Deliverables: STEP AP242 monolithic propeller, shaft-interface drawing, and a report listing mass in kg, max von Mises per case in MPa, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic 6061-T6 aluminum propeller for an ISR (International Submarine Races) human-powered submarine, compliant with ISR 18 Contestant Manual v1.2. Drive is a pedal crank at a design point of 120 RPM. 3 blades, pitch/diameter ratio 1.2, twist 45 deg at root to 15 deg at tip. Shaft interface is a 25 mm keyed bore (8 mm key). Rated continuous pilot power 300 W (23.9 N*m shaft torque at 120 RPM); peak sprint 600 W for 30 s (47.8 N*m).

Envelope: diameter 600 mm, hub diameter 60 mm, root chord 100 mm, tip chord 40 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 continuous operation: 23.9 N*m torque at 120 RPM, fatigue factor at blade-root fillet.
- LC2 sprint peak: 47.8 N*m torque.
- LC3 hydrodynamic thrust: ~150 N at 120 RPM and 2 m/s submarine speed, reacted through the hub.
- LC4 debris strike: 200 N tangential at the blade tip (transient quasi-static, ultimate-load case).
- LC5 centrifugal safety: 2 x sprint RPM = 240 RPM body load at the blade root.
- LC6 modal: cantilevered at the hub bore, first frequency >= 100 Hz (>> 6 Hz blade-passing at continuous).

prompt.title: ISR human-powered submarine propeller
prompt.brief: 3-blade 600 mm 6061-T6 propeller for pilot-powered submarine, continuous 300 W / sprint 600 W.

boundary_conditions.loads: torque via shaft, thrust along axis, tip impact, centrifugal body load

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Hydrodynamic thrust reduced to equivalent force.
